grammar.title: Section A: Grammar & Vocabulary
grammar.description: 30 multiple-choice questions • Choose ONE answer (A, B, C or D)

questions.question: Had I known about the roadworks, I ___ earlier.
questions.questionVi: Nếu tôi biết có sửa đường, tôi ___ sớm hơn.
questions.explanationVi: Đảo ngữ điều kiện loại 3 dùng 'would have + V3'.

questions.question: By the end of 2025, our team ___ the prototype for eighteen months.
questions.questionVi: Đến cuối 2025, nhóm chúng tôi ___ nguyên mẫu được 18 tháng.
questions.explanation: Future perfect continuous for duration before a future point.
questions.explanationVi: Tương lai hoàn thành tiếp diễn diễn tả khoảng thời gian trước mốc tương lai.

questions.question: He acted as though he ___ the decision single-handedly.
questions.questionVi: Anh ta cư xử như thể mình ___ quyết định một mình.
questions.explanation: 'As though' + past perfect for unreal past.
questions.explanationVi: 'As though' (không có thật quá khứ) → QKHT.

questions.question: Only after the audit ___ the accounting error.
questions.questionVi: Chỉ sau cuộc kiểm toán, ___ lỗi kế toán.
questions.explanation: Negative / limiting adverbials require inversion ('Only after... did').
questions.explanationVi: Sau 'Only after' phải đảo trợ động từ trước chủ ngữ.

questions.question: The board insisted that the CEO ___ the findings publicly.
questions.questionVi: Hội đồng yêu cầu CEO ___ công bố phát hiện.
questions.explanationVi: Câu mệnh lệnh dạng giả định dùng động từ nguyên mẫu không 'to'.

questions.question: She was on the ___ of accepting the offer when another email arrived.
questions.questionVi: Cô ấy đang trên ___ chấp nhận lời mời thì một email khác tới.
questions.explanationVi: Cụm cố định: 'on the verge of'.

questions.question: We’d better leave now; it looks as if it ___ any minute.
questions.explanation: 'As if' for imminent weather → 'is going to rain'.

questions.question: No sooner ___ the webinar than the server crashed.
The article was so densely written that few readers could make ___ of it.
Her proposal, together with several charts, ___ forwarded to the committee.
questions.explanation: ‘Together with’ ≠ plural subject; verb agrees with 'proposal'.

questions.question: If he ___ more assertive during negotiations, we might have secured a higher budget.
questions.explanation: Mixed third/second conditional about past = 'had been'.

questions.question: The consultant advised ___ market trends before investing.
questions.explanation: ‘Advise’ + gerund when no object stated: 'advised analysing'.

questions.question: Hardly any of the data ___ reliable enough for publication.
questions.explanation: 'Data' treated as uncountable here → singular verb.

questions.question: The sooner we finish the draft, ___ it can be sent to reviewers.
questions.explanation: Comparative correlative structure: 'The sooner..., the sooner...'.

questions.question: You mustn’t reveal the password to anyone, ___?
We were surprised at how few attendees showed up, ___ the extensive advertising campaign.
The finance manager objected to the figures ___ adjusted without approval.
The report is confidential and should ___ under any circumstances be shared externally.
She speaks three languages fluently, ___ English and Japanese.
Little ___ that their proposal had already been accepted.
Our manager prefers that all leave requests ___ through the HR portal.
The documentary provided a rarely-seen ___ into the daily life of deep-sea researchers.
I wish you ___ me before ordering the parts; we could have saved on shipping.
questions.explanation: Wish about past regret → past perfect.

questions.question: Neither the interns nor the project lead ___ aware of the schedule change.
questions.explanation: Verb agrees with nearest subject 'project lead' (singular).

questions.question: The research team could have avoided several pitfalls had they paid closer ___ to earlier studies.
He eventually came ___ with a solution that satisfied both sides.
Under no circumstances ___ personal devices be connected to the secure network.
The project took longer than expected, ___ not having sufficient staff.
After months of preparation, we are finally on the ___ of launching our first app.
The committee will meet next week, by which time the survey results ___ analysed.

reading.title: Section B: Reading Comprehension
reading.description: 20 multiple-choice questions • Read passages and choose the best answer

passages.title: Passage 1 – Offshore Wind Farm Debate
passages.content: Plans for an offshore wind farm 15 kilometres off the Verdant Coast have divided local residents. Proponents argue the 60-turbine array will supply one-third of the region’s electricity and create 120 construction jobs. Critics, however, warn that the installation could disrupt migratory bird routes and deter tourists who value the unbroken sea horizon. An independent environmental impact assessment concluded that blade-related bird fatalities would be ‘statistically negligible’ but did recommend a seasonal shutdown during peak migration weeks. Meanwhile, the regional tourism board commissioned a study showing that visitors cared more about hotel prices and beach cleanliness than distant turbines. A final vote by the coastal council is scheduled for October; if approved, the farm could be operational by 2029.

questions.question: What proportion of regional electricity would the farm supply?
Opponents of the project are MOST concerned about:
What did the environmental assessment recommend?
According to the tourism study, visitors prioritise:
If approved, the wind farm could be operational by:
The word 'array' in line 2 is closest in meaning to:
Which group commissioned a study on tourist preferences?
How many construction jobs are projected?
The tone of the passage can best be described as:
Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a potential impact?

passages.title: Passage 2 – Community Broadband Cooperative
passages.content: Frustrated by slow internet speeds, residents of Cedar Valley established a cooperative to build their own fibre-optic network. Members contributed an initial fee of $300 and agreed to volunteer eight hours for cable-laying. Within nine months, 78 percent of households were connected, enjoying average download speeds of 800 Mbps—ten times faster than the previous service. The project’s break-even model relies on reinvesting surplus subscription revenue to extend the fibre to outlying farms, thereby lowering each member’s long-term costs. While the cooperative has been praised for digital inclusivity, critics note that residents who cannot afford the upfront fee must wait until a hardship fund—currently half-funded—covers their connection. Nonetheless, regional planners view the scheme as a template for other underserved areas.

questions.question: What was the upfront membership fee?
Members were also required to:
Average download speeds after installation are approximately:
Surplus revenue is used to:
Why do some residents still lack a connection?
The hardship fund is currently:
What percentage of households are connected after nine months?
Critics of the cooperative focus mainly on:
Regional planners view the project as:
The word 'underserved' in the final sentence means:

listening.title: Section C: Listening Comprehension
listening.description: 10 multiple-choice questions • Listen to audio scripts and choose the best answer

scripts.title: Script 1 – Conference Call Reminder
scripts.content: Good morning, team. This is a reminder that our quarterly R&D call is tomorrow, Friday the 9th, at 08:00 London time, which is 3 p.m. in Ho Chi Minh City. The Zoom link in the agenda will activate ten minutes early for sound checks. Please upload your slide decks to the shared folder by 6 p.m. today so I can merge them. Remember to keep individual updates under five minutes; we have a hard stop at 09:15 because the finance department needs the room.

questions.question: When does the call begin in Ho Chi Minh City?
How early will the Zoom room open?
By what time must slides be uploaded?
What is the maximum length for each update?
Why must the meeting finish by 09:15 London time?

scripts.title: Script 2 – Library Closure Announcement
scripts.content: Attention patrons: Due to a scheduled software upgrade, the self-service checkout kiosks will be offline from 6 p.m. this evening until noon tomorrow. The reading rooms will remain open, but items must be borrowed through the main desk before 8 p.m. Tonight’s author talk has been relocated to Conference Hall B, which you can access via the east entrance. For real-time updates, follow the library’s social media channels.

questions.question: When will the kiosks go offline?
How long is the outage expected to last?
Until what time can items be borrowed at the main desk?
Where will the author talk take place?
Which entrance should attendees use for the talk?

writing.title: Section D: Writing
writing.description: 3 writing tasks • Suggested timing: 50–65 minutes total

tasks.title: Task 1 (120–140 words)
tasks.instruction: Write an email to your university’s accommodation office requesting an extension to your dormitory lease for an additional month. Explain your situation, propose a payment plan, and ask what documentation is needed.

tasks.title: Task 2 (170–200 words)
tasks.instruction: “University degrees are becoming less relevant in a world of online certificates and micro-credentials.” To what extent do you agree or disagree? Provide one main argument and support it with an example.

tasks.title: Task 3 (190–220 words)
tasks.instruction: A bar chart compares monthly subscriptions to three music-streaming platforms (StreamX, AudioWave, MelodyHub) from January to June: StreamX rises from 1.2 M to 2 M; AudioWave remains flat at ~1.1 M; MelodyHub climbs from 0.6 M to 1.3 M after a steep jump in April. Summarise the main trends and give one possible reason for MelodyHub’s sudden growth.
